title: Cephalocereus chrysostele
taxonomy: Cephalocereus chrysostele is a species of cactus in the family Cactaceae. It belongs to the genus Cephalocereus, which includes other species of cacti with columnar stems. The classification of Cephalocereus chrysostele is further detailed into the subfamily Cactoideae and the tribe Cacteae. Cephalocereus chrysostele is native to the Americas and is characterized by its distinctive columnar shape and white or yellow flowers. This species of cactus is often grown in cultivation for its unique appearance and hardy nature.

intro: Cephalocereus chrysostele is a species of cactus native to the Mexican state of San Luis Potosí, commonly known as the "Golden Spine Cactus". This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help to reduce stress, improve sleep quality, and boost the immune system. The bioactive constituents of C. chrysostele include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties.
